title: Setting the timezone within Alpine linux docker image
description: In a production environment, a time zone must be manually set up for reasons such as cron job, log time localization, and time synchronization betw...

children.value:
In a production environment, it may be necessary to set a time zone manually for reasons such as cron job, log time localization, and time synchronization between DB and OS.
The method of setting the time zone varies depending on the linux distribution, and in this article, we will try how to set it up in Alpine linux, which is often used to build a docker environment, and how to optimize it in docker.
Set up timezone in Alpine linux
Alpine Linux does not include packages related to time zone settings. Therefore, the
(Optional) I checked with the 'ls' command to see if it was installed properly.

props.code:
/app # ls -L /usr/share/zoneinfo/
Africa             CST6CDT            Etc                Greenwich          Kwajalein          PRC                UCT                posixrules
America            Canada             Europe             HST                Libya              PST8PDT            US                 right
Antarctica         Chile              Factory            Hongkong           MET                Pacific            UTC                zone.tab
Arctic             Cuba               GB                 Iceland            MST                Poland             Universal          zone1970.tab
Asia               EET                GB-Eire            Indian             MST7MDT            Portugal           W-SU
Atlantic           EST                GMT                Iran               Mexico             ROC                WET
Australia          EST5EDT            GMT+0              Israel             NZ                 ROK                Zulu
Brazil             Egypt              GMT-0              Jamaica            NZ-CHAT            Singapore          iso3166.tab
CET                Eire               GMT0               Japan              Navajo             Turkey             leap-seconds.list

children.value: Copy the proper zone info to localtime
When setting timezone in Docker, slightly deform the code mentioned above. When installing a package, use the
flag to disable cache, and perform capacity optimization by deleting packages that are no longer needed.

props.code:
FROM node:18.16.0-alpine3.17  

RUN apk add --no-cache tzdata && \
    cp /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime && \
    echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone && \
    apk del tzdata

children.value: Multi-stage builds is a feature supported since docker v17.06, and is a step-by-step function to exclude elements that are necessary for

When docker files become complicated, removing files that are not needed for result can be daunting.
If you use multi-stage buildings, you can focus only on the resources you need.

props.code:
FROM node:18.16.0-alpine3.17 AS base

# Builder process
FROM base AS builder

RUN apk add tzdata

# Runner process
FROM base AS runner

COPY --from=builder /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime
RUN echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone
